meta.title: The Teachings of Jesus Christ
meta.subtitle: A Comprehensive Categorization of His Words as Recorded in the New Testament
scope: The four Gospels (Matthew, Mark, Luke, John) — including parables, discourses, and dialogue
The institution of the Lord's Supper as quoted by Paul (1 Corinthians 11:23–25)
The letters to the seven churches and other direct speech in Revelation (Revelation 1–3, 22)
Damascus road appearance of Jesus to Saul (Acts 9:4–6; 22:7–10; 26:14–18)
Vision to Ananias commissioning Paul's mission (Acts 9:10–16)
Vision to Paul in the Temple (Acts 22:18–21)
Vision to Paul in Jerusalem custody (Acts 23:11)
Jesus speaks to Paul: 'My grace is sufficient for you' (2 Cor 12:9)
Heavenly voice to Peter on clean and unclean (Acts 10:13–15; 11:7–9)
Commission to write to the seven churches (Rev 1:11)
Jesus explains the vision of stars and lampstands (Rev 1:19–20)
Eschatological warning: 'I am coming like a thief' (Rev 16:15)
'Behold, I am making all things new' — new creation declaration (Rev 21:5–8)
Alpha and Omega self-declaration: 'I am the Alpha and the Omega... who is to come, the Almighty' (Rev 1:8)

teachings.text: If earthly fathers give good gifts, how much more will the heavenly Father give good things to those who ask
Not one sparrow falls without the Father's knowledge — even the hairs of your head are numbered; you are worth more
Your Father knows what you need before you ask; he sees what is done in secret and rewards it
Call no man your father on earth — you have one Father, who is in heaven
God is Spirit — those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth; the Father seeks such worshipers
God so loved the world he gave his Son — not to condemn the world but to save it; whoever believes will not perish
With God all things are possible; the Father raises the dead and gives life to whom he will
No one can come to the Son unless the Father draws him — all the Father gives will come; none can be snatched from his hand
The Father himself loves those who love Jesus; he has granted the Son authority over all judgment

categories.title: The Identity of Jesus Christ
categories.description: Jesus made explicit claims about his own nature, origin, authority, and mission that form the theological core of his teaching.

teachings.text: I AM the bread of life — whoever comes to me shall not hunger; whoever believes shall not thirst
I AM the light of the world — whoever follows me will not walk in darkness
I AM the door / gate — whoever enters through me will be saved
I AM the good shepherd — I know my sheep and lay down my life for them
I AM the resurrection and the life — whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live
I AM the way, the truth, and the life — no one comes to the Father except through me
I AM the true vine, and my Father is the vinedresser
Before Abraham was, I AM
I AM the First and the Last, the Living One — dead, and behold alive forevermore; I hold the keys of death and Hades
I AM the Alpha and the Omega, the root and offspring of David, the bright morning star

At his arrest in the garden, Jesus steps forward and twice declares 'I am he' in response to those seeking Jesus of Nazareth, and the power of the utterance causes the arresting party to draw back and fall to the ground.
Jesus declares himself to be the Alpha and the Omega — the first and the last, the beginning and the end — identifying himself as the Lord God who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.
I and the Father are one — the Father is in me and I am in the Father
He who has seen me has seen the Father — I do nothing on my own but speak as the Father taught me
The Father loves the Son and has given all things into his hand — all things have been delivered to me; no one knows the Son except the Father
The Father is greater than I; those who honor the Son honor the Father who sent him
The Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins and is Lord of the Sabbath
All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me
He has come not to abolish the Law but to fulfill it; to call sinners to repentance; to seek and save the lost; to give his life as a ransom for many
He came to cast fire on the earth — not peace but a sword; he is the one who searches mind and heart
He descended from heaven and is from above — not of this world; before Abraham was, I AM
He came from the Father into the world and now returns to the Father
He asks the Father to restore the glory they shared before the world existed

subcategories.title: Predictions of His Suffering, Death, and Resurrection

teachings.text: The Son of Man must suffer many things, be rejected, be killed, and rise on the third day
As Jonah was in the fish three days, so will the Son of Man be in the earth; destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it
As Moses lifted up the serpent, so must the Son of Man be lifted up — and when lifted, will draw all people
The good shepherd lays down his life of his own accord; unless a grain of wheat falls and dies, it cannot bear fruit
Everything written about him in the Prophets will be accomplished; the Christ must suffer and rise on the third day
He confirms to the high priest: I AM the Christ, the Son of God, coming on the clouds in power
He declares to the Samaritan woman: I who speak to you am the Messiah
The works done in the Father's name bear witness — if not doing the Father's works, do not believe
The blind receive sight, the lame walk, the dead are raised, the poor have good news — blessed is he who does not stumble over me
Jesus assures the repentant criminal crucified beside him that on that very day he will be with him in paradise, offering immediate salvation to one who turns to him in his final moments.

From the cross Jesus entrusts his mother to the care of the beloved disciple, saying to her 'Woman, behold your son' and to the disciple 'Behold your mother,' establishing a new family bond at the moment of his death.

At the ninth hour Jesus cries out with a loud voice the opening words of Psalm 22 — 'My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?' — giving voice to the full weight of divine abandonment he bears as the sin-bearer on the cross.
Jesus declares 'I thirst' from the cross, fulfilling Scripture and expressing the full physical humanity of the Son of God in his atoning suffering.

Jesus cries out 'It is finished' (tetelestai) as his final public word before death, declaring the complete accomplishment of his atoning work and the full discharge of every obligation his mission required.

With a loud voice Jesus entrusts his spirit to the Father in the words of Psalm 31:5, committing himself to God's care as he breathes his last and demonstrating that his death is a voluntary, trusting surrender into the Father's hands.

subcategories.title: The Promise and Coming of the Spirit

teachings.text: The Father will send another Helper / Advocate — the Spirit of truth, to be with them forever; he dwells with them and will be in them; the world cannot receive him
The Helper, sent in his name, will teach all things, bring his words to remembrance, and bear witness about him
It is to their advantage that he goes — if he does not, the Helper will not come; he will guide into all truth, speaking what he hears and glorifying Jesus
Wait for the promise of the Father — baptized with the Holy Spirit; you will receive power and be his witnesses; receive the Holy Spirit

subcategories.title: The Spirit's Work in the World

teachings.text: The Spirit will convict the world of sin (unbelief), righteousness (Jesus's return to the Father), and judgment (the ruler of this world condemned)
The Spirit gives life; the flesh profits nothing; out of the believer will flow rivers of living water
Your Father will give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him

subcategories.title: The Spirit in Witness and Trial

teachings.text: Do not be anxious about what to say when brought before rulers — the Spirit of the Father will speak through you in that hour
Every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven, but blasphemy against the Spirit will not — in this age or the age to come

categories.title: The Kingdom of God
categories.description: The Kingdom of God (or Kingdom of Heaven) is the organizing center of Jesus’s teaching. He describes its nature, how one enters it, its ethics, and its ultimate completion.

subcategories.title: Nature and Character of the Kingdom

teachings.text: Kingdom of heaven compared to a mustard seed — smallest of seeds grows into a large tree sheltering birds
Kingdom of heaven compared to leaven hidden in three measures of flour — permeates the whole
Kingdom compared to treasure hidden in a field, and to a merchant who sells everything for one pearl of great value — worth giving up everything
Kingdom compared to a net cast into the sea — gathers all kinds, sorted at the end of the age
Kingdom compared to a king who gave a wedding banquet — those invited refused, so the invitation went out to all
Kingdom of heaven compared to ten virgins awaiting the bridegroom — be ready
The kingdom does not come with outward observation — it is in your midst / among you
The kingdom of God is at hand — the time is fulfilled; repent and believe
The kingdom has been forcefully advancing since the days of John; it has been given to those who will produce its fruit
Many from east and west will come and recline at table in the kingdom

subcategories.title: Growth and Presence of the Kingdom

teachings.text: Seed growing secretly — a man scatters seed; it grows without his effort until harvest
The sower — the same seed falls on different soils and produces different responses to the kingdom message
Wheat and tares — kingdom and evil coexist until the final harvest, when angels will separate them
The mysteries of the kingdom are given to the disciples; to those outside, seeing they do not see and hearing they do not hear
Entry requires being born again — of water and Spirit; what is born of flesh is flesh; the Spirit moves like the wind
Unless righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, entry is impossible
Unless one becomes like a child, he cannot enter the kingdom
Not everyone who says “Lord, Lord” will enter — only he who does the will of the Father
Enter by the narrow gate — broad and easy is the way to destruction; few find the narrow way
It is hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom — easier for a camel through a needle's eye
Seek first the kingdom and his righteousness — all else will be added; many are called but few are chosen
The tax collectors and prostitutes enter the kingdom before the religious leaders who refused to believe

subcategories.title: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes

teachings.text: Blessed are the poor in spirit — theirs is the kingdom of heaven
Blessed are those who mourn — they shall be comforted
Blessed are the meek — they shall inherit the earth
Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness — they shall be satisfied
Blessed are the merciful — they shall receive mercy
Blessed are the pure in heart — they shall see God
Blessed are the peacemakers — they shall be called sons of God
Blessed are those persecuted for righteousness — theirs is the kingdom; rejoice, great is the reward in heaven
The parable of the two sons — one says no but goes, one says yes but does not go
The parable of the vineyard workers — all receive the same wage regardless of hours; the last shall be first
The parable of the wicked tenants — the vineyard taken from those who killed the owner's son
Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand — the time is fulfilled
He has come to call sinners to repentance, not the righteous; unless you repent you will all likewise perish
There is more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than ninety-nine who need no repentance
Across the letters to the churches: remember, be zealous, repent — or judgment will come

Jesus tells a parable of a fig tree that bears no fruit for three years: the owner wants to cut it down, but the gardener pleads for one more year to fertilize it, warning that if it still bears no fruit it will be cut down — urging immediate repentance before judgment falls.
Lost sheep — a shepherd leaves ninety-nine to search for the one; rejoices when found
Lost coin — a woman searches the whole house; calls her neighbors when found
Lost son — a son returns from ruin; his father runs to meet him and restores him fully
Come to me, all who are weary and burdened — take my yoke and learn from me; I am gentle and lowly; my yoke is easy and burden light
Whoever comes to me I will never cast out; behold, I stand at the door and knock — if anyone opens, I will come in
Entry into the kingdom requires being born again — of water and Spirit; flesh births flesh, Spirit births spirit
The Spirit moves like the wind — its origin and destination unseen; so it is with everyone born of the Spirit

subcategories.title: Faith as the Condition of Salvation

teachings.text: Whoever believes in the Son shall not perish but have eternal life and will not be judged; whoever rejects him will not see life
I am the way, the truth, and the life — no one comes to the Father except through me
I am the resurrection and the life — whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live; whoever lives and believes shall never die

subcategories.title: Eternal Life — Its Nature and Assurance

teachings.text: Eternal life is this: to know the only true God and Jesus Christ whom he sent
My sheep hear my voice — I know them, they follow me, I give them eternal life; no one can snatch them from my hand
Whoever eats his flesh and drinks his blood has eternal life and will be raised on the last day; whoever drinks the water he gives will never thirst again
In my Father's house are many rooms — he goes to prepare a place and will come again to receive his own
Enter by the narrow gate — broad and easy is the way to destruction; the narrow way leads to life and few find it
Not everyone who says “Lord, Lord” will enter — only he who does the will of the Father; many are called, few chosen

subcategories.title: The Nature and Power of Faith

teachings.text: Faith like a mustard seed can move mountains — nothing will be impossible; whatever is asked with belief will be received
According to your faith let it be done — your faith has saved / healed you
When the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth? Blessed are those who have not seen and yet believed
Why are you afraid? Why did you doubt? O you of little faith — do not be afraid, only believe
Do not be afraid, little flock — it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom; stop doubting and believe
Do not be anxious about life — food, drink, or clothing; life is more than these things; the Father knows what you need
Consider the birds the Father feeds and the lilies clothed better than Solomon — how much more will he clothe you
Seek first the kingdom and his righteousness — all else will be added; do not worry about tomorrow
Do not let your hearts be troubled — peace I leave with you; in this world you will have tribulation, but I have overcome the world
He has not come to abolish the Law but to fulfill it — not one jot or tittle will pass; whoever relaxes the least commandment will be least in the kingdom
The Law and the Prophets were until John — since then the kingdom is proclaimed
All the Law and the Prophets hang on loving God and loving neighbor — do this and you will live

subcategories.title: Moses and the Scriptures Pointing to Jesus

teachings.text: If they believed Moses, they would believe him — Moses wrote about him; as Moses lifted up the serpent, so must the Son of Man be lifted up
Abraham rejoiced to see his day; the Scriptures bear witness about him — yet they refuse to come to him
He earnestly desired to eat this Passover before his suffering — he will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom
Take and eat — this is my body given for you; do this in remembrance of me
This cup is my blood of the covenant — the new covenant in my blood, poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins; do this in remembrance of me
I will not drink of this fruit of the vine again until that day I drink it new in my Father's kingdom

subcategories.title: The Bread of Life — A Connected Teaching

teachings.text: I am the living bread that came down from heaven — whoever comes will not hunger; whoever believes will not thirst; this bread is my flesh given for the life of the world
Whoever eats his flesh and drinks his blood has eternal life — his flesh is true food, his blood is true drink; they abide in him and he in them
The Spirit gives life; the flesh profits nothing — the words he speaks are spirit and life

subcategories.title: New and Old — The Covenant Transition

teachings.text: Jesus teaches that new wine must be poured into new wineskins, for pouring it into old wineskins bursts them and loses both wine and skins — illustrating that the new covenant cannot be contained within the structures of the old.

subcategories.title: The Lord's Prayer — Pattern for Prayer

teachings.text: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name
Your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven
Give us this day our daily bread
Forgive us our debts / sins as we also forgive our debtors
Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil
Do not pray like hypocrites who stand in public to be seen — go into your room, shut the door, pray in secret
Do not heap up empty phrases as the Gentiles do — your Father knows what you need before you ask
When you stand praying, forgive if you hold anything against anyone
Ask and it will be given; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened — everyone who asks receives
The persistent friend at midnight — a man gives bread not out of friendship but because of shameless persistence
The persistent widow and the unjust judge — men ought always to pray and not lose heart; God will give justice to his elect
Whatever you ask in his name he will do — the Father may be glorified in the Son; ask and you will receive, that your joy may be full
He chose them to go and bear fruit — that whatever they ask the Father in his name he may give
If two of you agree on earth about anything they ask, it will be done — where two or three are gathered in his name, he is there
Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation — the spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak
He is the true vine, the Father is the vinedresser — every fruitless branch is removed; every fruitful branch is pruned to bear more
Abide in him as the branch in the vine — apart from him you can do nothing; whoever does not abide withers and is burned
If his words abide in you, ask whatever you wish; the Father is glorified in much fruit — this is how they prove to be his disciples
As the Father has loved him, so has he loved them — abide in his love by keeping his commandments, as he kept the Father's
These things he has spoken so that his joy may be in them and their joy may be full

categories.title: The High Priestly Prayer
categories.description: This chapter records Jesus praying aloud before his arrest — his intercession for himself, his disciples, and all who will believe.

subcategories.title: Prayer for Himself — The Hour Has Come

teachings.text: Father, the hour has come — glorify your Son that he may glorify you; he has authority to give eternal life to all you have given him
Eternal life is to know the only true God and Jesus Christ whom he sent; he has glorified the Father and asks to be restored to the glory shared before the world existed
He has manifested the Father's name to those given to him; he prays for them — not for the world — that they be kept united and protected from the evil one
Sanctify them in the truth — your word is truth; as the Father sent him into the world, he sends them; for their sake he consecrates himself
He prays for all who will believe through their word — that they may be one as the Father and Son are one; their unity is the testimony that the Father sent him
The glory given to him he has given them; he desires that they be with him to see his glory; he has made the Father's name known so that his love may be in them
Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength — this is the great and first commandment; all the Law and the Prophets hang on this and love of neighbor
Love your neighbor as yourself — the second commandment, like the first
The Good Samaritan — a despised outsider shows mercy to the man left for dead; go and do likewise
Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you, pray for those who persecute you — be merciful as the Father is merciful
If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Even tax collectors do the same; lend expecting nothing in return
Do to others as you would have them do to you — this sums up the Law and the Prophets
A new commandment: love one another as I have loved you — by this all will know you are his disciples
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down his life for his friends; you are his friends if you do what he commands
He no longer calls them servants but friends — he has made known everything he heard from the Father

categories.title: Righteousness and Ethics
categories.description: This category covers the ethical teachings of the Sermon on the Mount (Matthew 5–7) and Sermon on the Plain (Luke 6), including Jesus’s radical internalization of righteousness.

teachings.text: You are the salt of the earth — if it loses its saltiness it is worthless
You are the light of the world — a city on a hill cannot be hidden; let your light shine so others may glorify the Father
He has not come to abolish the Law but to fulfill it — not one jot or tittle will pass; whoever relaxes even the least commandment will be least in the kingdom
Righteousness must exceed that of the scribes and Pharisees to enter the kingdom

subcategories.title: The Antitheses — “You Have Heard… But I Say to You”

teachings.text: Anger: murder begins in the heart — contempt and insult bring one into judgment
Lust: adultery begins with a lustful look; if eye or hand causes sin, remove it
Divorce: a certificate of divorce causes adultery, except for sexual immorality
Oaths: do not swear at all — let your yes be yes and your no be no; anything more is from evil
Retaliation: do not resist the evil one — turn the other cheek, give your cloak, go the extra mile
Enemies: love, pray for, and do good to enemies — be perfect as the Father is perfect
Give in secret, not before others to be seen — your Father who sees in secret will reward you
Fast without showing it — anoint your head, wash your face; true piety is seen by God alone
Do not judge, or you will be judged — first remove the log from your own eye, then help your brother with his
Do not give what is holy to dogs or pearls to pigs; judge with righteous judgment, not by appearances

subcategories.title: The Golden Rule and Two Ways

teachings.text: Whatever you wish others would do to you, do also to them — this is the Law and the Prophets
He who hears his words and does them is like a wise man who built on rock; who hears but does not act built on sand

subcategories.title: The Nature of Greatness in the Kingdom

teachings.text: Whoever would be great must be a servant — whoever would be first must be slave of all; the last will be first
Everyone who exalts himself will be humbled; whoever humbles himself will be exalted; the greatest is the servant of all
Whoever receives a child in his name receives him — the least among all is the greatest
The Son of Man came not to be served but to serve and give his life as a ransom — he is among them as one who serves
After washing their feet: I have given you an example — as I have done, so you must do; a servant is not greater than his master; blessed are you if you do these things
Do not take the seat of honor — sit in the lowest place; when you give a banquet, invite those who cannot repay you
The Pharisee and the tax collector — self-righteousness condemned; humility is justified
Unless you turn and become like children you cannot enter the kingdom — whoever humbles himself like a child is greatest; whoever does not receive the kingdom like a child will not enter

subcategories.title: The Power and Accountability of Words

teachings.text: People will give account for every careless word on judgment day — by your words you will be justified or condemned
What comes out of the mouth proceeds from the heart — this is what defiles a person
Do not take oaths at all — not by heaven, earth, Jerusalem, or your own head; let your yes be yes and your no be no
I am the way, the truth, and the life — he was born and came into the world to bear witness to the truth; everyone on the side of truth listens to him
His testimony is valid — he knows where he came from; two witnesses confirm truth: himself and the Father
You will know the truth and the truth will set you free; if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed
He who has ears to hear, let him hear — a repeated call to spiritual attention throughout his ministry
The mysteries of the kingdom are given to the disciples — to others, seeing they do not see and hearing they do not hear; blessed are eyes that see and ears that hear
Pay attention to what you hear — to the one who has, more will be given; from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken
The Pharisees are blind guides of the blind — both fall into a pit; you can read the sky but not the signs of the times
Because you say “I see” — your guilt remains; you search the Scriptures thinking they give life, yet refuse to come to him
Beware of false prophets in sheep's clothing — you will know them by their fruits; a good tree cannot bear bad fruit
Beware of false christs and false prophets who perform great signs to deceive, if possible, even the elect
Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy

Jesus warns that when an unclean spirit leaves a person it wanders seeking rest, finds none, and returns to find the house swept and put in order; it then brings seven spirits more evil than itself, leaving that person's final state worse than the first.
Nothing is covered that will not be uncovered — what is whispered in private will be proclaimed on the housetops
A lamp is not brought in to be hidden — nothing is secret except to come to light
Your sins are forgiven — the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins
The one forgiven much loves much — the one forgiven little loves little
Forgive and you will be forgiven — if you do not forgive others, your Father will not forgive you
Forgive not seven times but seventy-seven / seventy times seven — without limit
The unforgiving servant — forgiven a vast debt, he refuses to forgive a small one
Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do
Leave your gift at the altar — first be reconciled to your brother, then come and offer it
If your brother sins against you, go privately — if he listens, you have won him; take witnesses; then tell the church
Settle matters quickly with your adversary while on the way
From the beginning God made them male and female — what God has joined, let no one separate; Moses permitted divorce only because of hardness of heart
Whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another commits adultery
In the resurrection, people neither marry nor are given in marriage — they are like angels

Jesus teaches that some people are incapable of marriage by birth, some by human action, and some have renounced marriage for the sake of the kingdom of heaven, and he calls those who are able to receive this word to do so.
Whoever looks with lustful intent has already committed adultery in his heart; if eye or hand causes sin, remove it
What comes out of a person defiles him — from within come sexual immorality, adultery, and licentiousness
Honor your father and mother; yet whoever loves family more than Jesus is not worthy of him
He came not to bring peace but division — households will be divided; whoever does the will of God is his true family
Those who leave family or possessions for the kingdom will receive many times more, with eternal life
Let the little children come — do not hinder them; to such belongs the kingdom of heaven
Whoever causes one of these little ones to stumble — better a millstone around his neck; do not despise them
Do not store up earthly treasure — store up in heaven; where your treasure is, your heart will be also
The eye is the lamp of the body — if your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light
No one can serve two masters — you cannot serve both God and money
The rich fool — his soul is required the very night he builds bigger barns; life does not consist in abundance of possessions
Woe to the rich and full — they have received their consolation
It is hard for the rich to enter the kingdom — easier for a camel through a needle's eye; with God all things are possible
Sell what you possess, give to the poor, and follow — store up treasure in heaven
Give to the one who begs; lend expecting nothing in return
The parable of the talents / minas — faithful stewardship multiplies what is given; unfaithfulness forfeits it
The dishonest manager — use worldly wealth wisely; one faithful in little is faithful in much
The widow's offering — she gave all she had out of poverty; it is more blessed to give than to receive
The rich man feasts while Lazarus lies at his gate — in the afterlife their positions are reversed; a great chasm is fixed
If they do not hear Moses and the Prophets, they will not be convinced even if someone rises from the dead

subcategories.title: The Blessing on the Poor and Woe to the Rich

teachings.text: Blessed are the poor — the kingdom of God is theirs; blessed are those who hunger; the poor have good news preached to them
Woe to the rich and full — they have received their consolation; woe to those who laugh now

subcategories.title: The Judgment of the Nations — Sheep and Goats

teachings.text: The Son of Man separates all nations as a shepherd separates sheep from goats — those who served the hungry, thirsty, stranger, naked, sick, and imprisoned served him
Whatever was done for the least of these was done for him; whatever was not done for the least was not done for him
Blessed are the merciful — they shall receive mercy; I desire mercy, not sacrifice
The Good Samaritan — the one who showed mercy is the true neighbor; go and do likewise
You neglect justice and the love of God — these ought to have been practiced without neglecting the others

Jesus silences his opponents by declaring that Caesar's image on a coin means Caesar's things belong to Caesar, while God's things belong to God, establishing distinct but complementary obligations to earthly authority and to God.
Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men — come, follow me
Foxes have holes and birds have nests — the Son of Man has no place to lay his head; let the dead bury their own dead
No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for the kingdom

Jesus gently rebukes the distracted Martha and declares that her sister Mary has chosen the one thing necessary — sitting at his feet and listening to his word — and that it will not be taken from her.
Anyone who would follow must deny himself, take up his cross daily, and follow — the cost is total self-surrender
Whoever saves his life will lose it — whoever loses it for his sake will find it; what profits a man to gain the world and forfeit his soul?
Anyone who does not renounce family, possessions, and even his own life cannot be a disciple
The tower builder and the king going to war — count the cost before you begin
A good tree bears good fruit; a bad tree cannot — by their fruits you shall know them; every fruitless tree is cut down
He who abides in him bears much fruit — apart from him you can do nothing; the Father is glorified by much fruit
He chose them to go and bear fruit that abides; if his words abide in them, they may ask whatever they wish
The one who endures to the end will be saved; be faithful unto death and I will give you the crown of life
Hold fast what you have until he comes — I am coming soon; let no one seize your crown
To the one who conquers: promised rewards include the tree of life, hidden manna, authority over nations, white garments, a name in the book of life, a pillar in the temple, a place on his throne

All authority in heaven and earth has been given — go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing in the name of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all he commanded; he is with them always
As the Father sent him, so he sends them — receive the Holy Spirit; you will be his witnesses to the ends of the earth
Blessed are those persecuted for righteousness — blessed when people revile and persecute; rejoice, for great is the reward in heaven
If they persecuted him, they will persecute his followers — a servant is not greater than his master; you will be hated by all for his name
They will be put out of synagogues; the hour is coming when killing them will seem like worship of God
Do not fear those who kill the body but not the soul — fear him who can destroy both; not a hair of your head will perish
In the world they will have tribulation — but take heart; he has overcome the world; by endurance they will gain their lives

Jesus tells Paul that his grace is sufficient for him and that his power is made perfect in weakness, reframing Paul's thorn in the flesh as the very condition in which divine strength is most fully displayed.
Anyone who would follow must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow — whoever saves his life will lose it; whoever loses it for his sake will save it
What does it profit a man to gain the whole world and forfeit his soul?

subcategories.title: The Seven Woes to Scribes and Pharisees (Matthew 23)

teachings.text: Woe: they shut the kingdom of heaven in people's faces — they neither enter nor let others in
Woe: they travel far to make one convert, then make him twice the child of hell
Woe: blind guides who strain out a gnat but swallow a camel — neglecting what actually sanctifies
Woe: they tithe mint and dill but neglect justice, mercy, and faithfulness
Woe: they clean the outside of the cup while inside is greed and self-indulgence
Woe: they are like whitewashed tombs — beautiful outside, full of dead bones within
Woe: they build tombs for prophets while being sons of those who killed them; brood of vipers — how will they escape hell?
They devour widows' houses and make long prayers for a show
These people honor him with their lips but their hearts are far from him — in vain they worship, teaching human traditions
It is not what goes into a person that defiles him — what defiles comes from within: evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, slander, arrogance
The Pharisee and the tax collector — self-righteousness condemned; humility justified

subcategories.title: Traditions of Men Versus God's Commands

teachings.text: They reject God's commands to keep their own traditions — the tradition of Corban nullifies the command to honor parents
They have made void the word of God for the sake of tradition — Isaiah prophesied rightly about them
The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath — the Son of Man is Lord of it
It is lawful to do good on the Sabbath — would you not rescue an animal or a child? This woman bound by Satan ought to be loosed
The scribes and Pharisees sit on Moses's seat — do what they say but not what they do; they burden others but will not lift a finger
They do all their deeds to be seen — they love places of honor, greetings in marketplaces, and titles
On this rock he will build his church — the gates of hell shall not prevail; he gives the keys of the kingdom
Whatever is bound on earth will be bound in heaven; whatever is loosed on earth will be loosed in heaven
If your brother sins, go privately — if he listens, you have won him; take witnesses; then tell the church; treat the unrepentant as an outsider
Where two or three are gathered in his name, there he is among them; if two agree about anything they ask, it will be done
He prays that they all may be one as the Father and Son are one — that the world may believe that the Father sent him
A kingdom divided against itself cannot stand
The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few — pray earnestly to the Lord to send out laborers

subcategories.title: Instructions for the Twelve and the Seventy

teachings.text: Go and proclaim: the kingdom of heaven is at hand; heal the sick, raise the dead, cast out demons — freely give
Take no bag, no purse — the worker deserves his wages; if a town welcomes, stay; if not, shake the dust off your feet
Behold, he sends them as sheep among wolves — be wise as serpents and innocent as doves
What is whispered, proclaim on the housetops; do not fear those who kill the body — fear him who can destroy both soul and body
Everyone who acknowledges him before men, he will acknowledge before the Father; whoever denies him, he will deny
They will be dragged before governors and kings — the gospel must first be proclaimed to all nations
Brother will betray brother; they will be hated by all for his name's sake — he who endures to the end will be saved
Not a hair of your head will perish — by your endurance you will gain your lives

subcategories.title: Visions and Commissions to the Apostles

teachings.text: In a vision Jesus instructs Ananias to go to Saul of Tarsus, assuring him that Saul is praying and has seen a vision of Ananias coming to restore his sight, and reveals that Saul is a chosen instrument to carry his name before Gentiles, kings, and the people of Israel.

Jesus appears to Paul in a night vision in Corinth, commanding him not to be afraid but to speak and not be silent, promising that no one will attack him to harm him because many people in the city belong to him.
While Paul is praying in the Temple in a trance, Jesus urges him to leave Jerusalem quickly because the people there will not accept his testimony, and redirects his witness to the Gentiles far away.
The Lord stands by Paul in his Jerusalem custody and encourages him, confirming that just as he has testified about him in Jerusalem he must also testify in Rome.

A heavenly voice commands Peter to rise, kill, and eat from a sheet of unclean animals, and when Peter protests it is profane, the voice declares that what God has made clean he must not call common — an event Peter later interprets as God's directive to receive Gentiles into the gospel.
Why are you troubled? See my hands and feet — touch me and see; a spirit has no flesh and bones; everything written about him in Moses, the Prophets, and Psalms must be fulfilled
The Christ must suffer and rise on the third day — repentance and forgiveness of sins should be proclaimed to all nations; they are witnesses of these things
Peace be with you — as the Father has sent him, so he sends them; receive the Holy Spirit; those whose sins you forgive are forgiven

The risen Jesus appears to Saul on the Damascus road, identifies himself as Jesus whom Saul is persecuting, commissions him to rise and enter the city, and later in Paul's own account extends the commission to bear his name before Gentiles, kings, and the people of Israel.
Put your finger here, see his hands, reach into his side — do not disbelieve but believe; blessed are those who have not seen and yet believe
Three times: do you love me? — feed my lambs, tend my sheep, feed my sheep; love demonstrated through care of others
When you are old, another will lead you where you do not want to go — follow me; what is it to you if another remains? You follow me
All authority has been given — go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing and teaching them to observe all his commands; he is with them always, to the end of the age
Wait in Jerusalem for the promised Spirit — not to know times and seasons, but to receive power and be his witnesses to the ends of the earth

categories.title: Eschatology and the End Times
categories.description: The Olivet Discourse (Matthew 24–25, Mark 13, Luke 21) is the primary source for this category.

teachings.text: Watch out that no one deceives you — many will come claiming to be the Christ; do not be led astray
Wars, famines, earthquakes, and signs in the heavens — the beginning of birth pains, not yet the end
The gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world — then the end will come
The abomination of desolation in the holy place — let those in Judea flee; then great tribulation such as has never been
False christs and false prophets will show great signs to deceive, if possible, even the elect
The sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light, the stars will fall — the sign of the Son of Man will appear

subcategories.title: The Return of the Son of Man

teachings.text: The Son of Man will come on the clouds with power and great glory — his angels will gather the elect from the four winds
As lightning flashes from east to west, so will his coming be — as sudden and unexpected as the days of Noah and Lot
One will be taken and one left

From the throne the one who sits on it declares that he is making all things new, commands John to write that these words are trustworthy and true, announces that it is done and that he is the Alpha and the Omega, promises free water of life to the thirsty and an inheritance to the conqueror, and warns that the cowardly and unbelieving will face the lake that burns with fire and sulfur.
Watch — you do not know the day or hour; be on guard that hearts are not weighed down with the cares of life
The faithful and unfaithful servant — who gives food at the proper time versus who abuses his master's absence
The ten virgins — five wise with oil prepared, five foolish who ran out; the door was shut — be ready
I am coming soon — blessed is the one who keeps these words; behold, I come quickly

Jesus interjects into the bowl-judgment narrative with a warning that he is coming like a thief and pronounces a blessing on those who stay awake and keep their garments so they will not be found naked and exposed to shame.

subcategories.title: The Fig Tree and the Unknown Hour

teachings.text: From the fig tree learn its lesson — when the branch is tender, summer is near; when these things happen, the end is near
Heaven and earth will pass away, but his words will not; no one knows that day or hour — not the angels, not even the Son

subcategories.title: The Certainty and Scope of Judgment

teachings.text: The Son of Man will come in glory with his angels and repay each according to his works; the Father has given all judgment to the Son
People will give account for every careless word; the word he has spoken will judge those who reject him on the last day
It will be more tolerable for Sodom than for those who reject his messengers; all in the tombs will come out — the good to life, the evil to judgment
Fear him who can destroy both soul and body in hell — better to enter life maimed than go to hell with unquenchable fire
Outer darkness — weeping and gnashing of teeth; Gehenna of fire
Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil — eternal punishment versus eternal life
The dead will hear the voice of the Son of God and live — I am the resurrection and the life; I will raise him up on the last day
Many will come from east and west to recline at table in the kingdom with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob

categories.title: The Seven Churches
categories.description: Spoken directly by the risen Christ. Each letter follows a pattern: what he knows about the church, what concerns he raises, a call to repentance or faithfulness, and a promise to the one who overcomes.

subcategories.title: Introduction to the Seven Churches — The Vision of Christ

teachings.text: The risen Jesus commands John to write in a book what he sees and send it to the seven churches: Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.

Jesus instructs John to write what he has seen, what is, and what is to take place after these things, and explains that the seven stars in his right hand are the angels of the seven churches and the seven golden lampstands are the seven churches themselves.

subcategories.title: Ephesus — The Church That Left Its First Love

teachings.text: Commends their toil, endurance, and testing of false apostles — but they have abandoned their first love; remember, repent, and return to those works
To the one who conquers: I will grant to eat of the tree of life in the paradise of God
I know your tribulation and poverty — but you are rich; do not fear what you are about to suffer; be faithful unto death
To the one who conquers: the crown of life; will not be hurt by the second death
You have held fast my name and not denied my faith — but some hold the teaching of Balaam and practice sexual immorality; repent or I will war against them
To the one who conquers: hidden manna and a white stone with a new name

subcategories.title: Thyatira — The Church Tolerating False Teaching

teachings.text: I know your love, faith, service, and endurance — but you tolerate Jezebel, who teaches immorality; I gave her time to repent; I search mind and heart and will give each what his works deserve
Hold fast what you have until I come; to the one who conquers: authority over the nations and the morning star
You have a reputation of being alive but are dead — wake up and strengthen what remains; remember, keep it, and repent; if not, I will come like a thief
To the one who conquers: clothed in white; name not blotted from the book of life; acknowledged before the Father
I have set before you an open door no one can shut; you have kept my word and not denied my name; I will keep you from the hour of trial
I am coming soon — hold fast so no one seizes your crown; to the one who conquers: a pillar in God's temple with the name of God and the new Jerusalem
You are neither cold nor hot — because you are lukewarm I will spit you out; you say “I need nothing” but you are wretched, poor, blind, and naked; those I love I rebuke — be zealous and repent
Behold, I stand at the door and knock — if anyone opens I will come in; to the one who conquers: I will grant to sit with me on my throne
